title: Few-Shot Knowledge Distillation of LLMs With Counterfactual Explanations

repos.name: FaisalHamman__CoD
repos.evidence: README title is 'Few-Shot Knowledge Distillation of LLMs With Counterfactual Explanations (CoD)'; lists authors Faisal Hamman, Pasan Dissanayake, Yanjun Fu, Sanghamitra Dutta at UMD — exact match to paper authors. README states 'Published at the 39th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2025)'. Paper text (line 27) says 'Our code is available at https://github.com/FaisalHamman/CoD'. Repo contains cfx-generator/ (counterfactual generation), text-classification/ (KD/LWD/TED distillation scripts), scripts_cfx/ (run orchestration) — all named after the paper's core method COD.

rationale: The single cloned repo FaisalHamman__CoD is the paper's own official implementation, linked directly by the paper's abstract ('Our code is available at https://github.com/FaisalHamman/CoD'). The README title, author list, and NeurIPS 2025 venue all match the paper exactly. The repo owner (FaisalHamman) is the paper's first author. Directory structure maps to the paper's framework: cfx-generator/ for CFE generation, text-classification/ for distillation, scripts_cfx/ for orchestration. All audit findings are written against this correct repo and are substantive methodology/reproducibility findings (no 'no code' claim). The audit was done correctly against the right repository.
audit_impact: None — the audit correctly identified the paper's own repo and wrote all findings against the correct code. No misclassification or contamination. All 8 findings in findings_verified.json have verdict='keep' and are substantive (missing teacher-validation of CFEs, missing ablation drivers, eval-on-validation vs. test-set discrepancy, model-selection on the reported split). Audit verdict is correct.
recommended_action: No action needed. The repo is the paper's own core implementation. Findings stand as written.
